MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Albert Lee defendant in propria persona
Leah Hess for plaintiff and opposing party: Primo Segundo, N. V. a Netherlands Antilles Corporation

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Michelle Reddington's motion for protective order regarding:
a) Lee's request for production of documents to Reddington (set #4)
b) Lee's special interrogatories to Reddington (set #2)
c) Lee's request for production of documents to Hess (set #3)
d) Lee's special interrogatories to Hess (set #2)
e) Lee's request for admissions to Reddington (set #3)
f) Lee's form interrogatories to Reddington (set #4)
g) Lee's request for admissions to Hess (set #3)
h) Lee's form interrogatories to Hess (set #3)

Denied at this time. Counsel for Albert Lee shall refrain from propounding discovery in successive requests. The Court will entertain a motion for a protective order if further discovery is cumulative or unnecessary.
Answers to written discovery by 2/20/07. Deposition by 2/28/07. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Leah Hess for defendant and moving party: Michelle Reddington
Albert Lee defendant in Propia Persona

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted, with modification regarding date.
Defendant MICHELLE REDDINGTON'S motion for a protective order regarding is granted in part. The Court orders that all discovery in this action is stayed until January 26, 2007. Neither party may propound nor is required to respond to any outstanding discovery until further order of the Court. Defendant Hess and Reddington's motion as to the written discovery propounded by Albert Lee, as well as the motion to compel by Albert Lee currently scheduled for January 26, 2006 are both continued to January 29, 2007 at 10:30 a.m. on the court's own motion. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
